name: mxgap

metadata.name: mxgap
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: ML models to predict terminated MXene bandgaps.

documentation:
# MXgap: A Machine Learning Program to predict MXene Bandgaps

[![PyPi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mxgap)](https://pypi.org/project/mxgap/) [![Tests](https://github.com/diegonti/mxgap/actions/workflows/python_tests.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/diegonti/mxgap/actions/workflows/python_tests.yaml) ## About `mxgap` is a computational tool designed to streamline electronic structure calculations for MXenes using hybrid functionals like PBE0. By employing Machine Learning (ML) models, mxgap predicts the PBE0 bandgap based on features extracted from a PBE calculation. Here’s a detailed overview of its functionality: ### 1. Feature Extraction: - Automatically extracts essential features and key data from a PBE calculation output, specifically tailored for [VASP](https://www.vasp.at/) (Vienna *Ab initio* Simulation Package) outputs. - It leverages the structural information from the CONTCAR file, and optionally users can choose to include the density of states (DOS) from the DOSCAR file to enhance prediction accuracy, depending on the selected ML model. - The program is designed for periodic systems. So, currently the tool requires a *p*($1\times1$) terminated MXene unit cell in the CONTCAR file for proper functionality. ### 2. ML Prediction: - Uses trained ML models to predict bandgap values, reducing the computational cost associated with performing full PBE0 calculations. - Several ML models have been trained and are available to use. The default (and best) one is a combination of a Classifier (GBC) that discriminates metallic or semiconductor MXenes and a Regressor (RFR, trained with semiconductor MXenes) to predict the bandgap. More info about the ML models in the [models/](mxgap/models/) folder. ### 3. Output: - Generates a report file, `mxgap.info`, which contains the ML predictions and results.
This program is based on the data gathered in our works: [*J. Mater. Chem. A*, 2023, 11, 13754-13764](https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA01933K) and [*Energy Environ. Mater.*, 2024, 7, e12774](https://doi.org/10.1002/eem2.12774). ## Installation `mxgap` works for python >= 3.9, and can be installed using the Python package manager `pip`: ``` pip install mxgap ``` If you use conda/anaconda, the safest thing to do is to create a new environment and then install `mxgap`: ``` conda create -n mxgap python conda activate mxgap pip install mxgap ``` If you wish, you can install the latest version of `mxgap` from GitHub source with the commands below: ``` git clone https://github.com/diegonti/mxgap.git cd mxgap pip install . ``` If installed via `pip`, it should handle all the dependencies, but If you encounter issues, install the exact package versions specified in requirements.txt using: ``` pip install -r requirements.txt ``` ## Usage The program is mainly used through the CLI: ``` mxgap [-h] [-f CONTCAR [DOSCAR]] [-m MODEL] [PATH] ``` With the arguments and options explained below: ``` positional arguments: path Specify the path to the directory containing the calculation output files, if empty, will select the current directory. Must contain at least the optimized CONTCAR file and, for DOS-trained models, the PBE DOSCAR file. options: -h, --help Show this help message and exit. -f FILES [FILES ...], --files FILES [FILES ...] Specify in order the paths to the CONTCAR and DOSCAR (if needed) files manually. The path positional argument has preference over this. -m MODEL, --model MODEL Choose the trained MXene-Learning model to use. By default, the most accurate version is selected (GBC+RFR_onlygap). -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT Path of the output file. By default it will generate a mxgap.info in the CONTCAR folder. -l, --list List of all trained ML models available to choose. ``` So, for a quick example, the below command will look for the CONTCAR and DOSCAR files in the specified folder and use the default (best) ML model to predict the bandgap: ``` mxgap examples/folder/ ``` Or using the `-f` option to specify both CONTCAR and DOSCAR files: ``` mxgap -f path/to/CONTCAR path/to/DOSCAR ``` Also, the program can be imported as a python module. See the [Jupyter Notebook](tutorials/tutorials.ipynb) for some tutorials. Here is a quick example: ```python from mxgap import run_prediction path = "examples/La2C1Cl2/" model = "GBC+RFR" prediction = run_prediction(path, model = model) ``` ## Tests The program has been tested using [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/diegonti/mxgap/blob/master/.github/workflows/python_tests.yaml) for Windows, Ubuntu and MacOS with python versions >=3.9. You can run tests locally using `pytest` in the project folder: ``` cd mxgap pytest ``` ## Help To get information about the program and its use, run the command: ``` mxgap -h ``` For any more doubts or questions, feel free to contact [me](mailto:diegonti.doc@gmail.com).

modules.name: mxgap.utils
documentation:
General utility functions that the MXgap program uses.


Diego Ontiveros

functions.name: mxgap.utils.print2
documentation: Print text both into screen and file.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.print_predictions
documentation: Print predictions to the output file.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.load_pickle
documentation: Reads the data of a saved pickle object.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.add_path_ending
documentation: Adds / to ending of path if needed.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.is_iterable
documentation: Assess if an object is iterable.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.is_close
documentation: Assess if two arrays are close, within a certain tolerance.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.round_zeros
documentation: Sets to actual zero the values close to zero in an aray, with a given tolerance.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.get_structure_indices
documentation: Gets the structure indices from the given stack and hollows.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.load_models_list
documentation: Loads available models list from file.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.load_normalization
documentation: Loads the min/max normalization parameters used when training the models.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.model_needsDOS
documentation: Checks if the selected model needs the DOSCAR file.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.model_type
documentation: Return the model type (C or R).

functions.name: mxgap.utils.reorder_model_list
documentation: Returns a ordered model_list, following C+R order.

functions.name: mxgap.utils.rescale
documentation: Rescales output, depending on the model case (bandgap or VBM/CBM).

functions.name: mxgap.test.test_.get_test_info
documentation: Creates a dictionary with the test MXene and information to test.

modules.name: mxgap.cli
documentation:
Main program for the MXap package utilization through CLI.
Use MXene-trained ML models to predict bandgap.

Diego Ontiveros - 07/10/2024 [diego.ontiveros@ub.edu]

functions.name: mxgap.cli.cli
documentation: Command Line Interface. Get user inputs from terminal (ArgParse) and feed them to the main ML prediction.

modules.name: mxgap.input
documentation:
General user input handler and validation functions that the MXgap program uses.


Diego Ontiveros

functions.name: mxgap.input.parse_user_input
documentation: Uses ArgumentParser to get the user arguments and provide information.

functions.name: mxgap.input.input_path_exists
documentation: Asserts that the paths given by the user exist.

functions.name: mxgap.input.model_exists
documentation: Checks if the given model exists in the available list.

functions.name: mxgap.input.validate_user_input
documentation:
Validates the input given by the user. Checks input incompatibility, errors, etc.
If valid, returns the CONTCAR, DOSCAR, and output paths.

functions.name: mxgap.input.validate_user_files
documentation: Validates the CONTCAR and DOSCAR files given by the user.

modules.name: mxgap.ML
documentation:
Functions related to perform the ML prediction.

Diego Ontiveros

functions.name: mxgap.ML.model_prediction
documentation: Loads model and makes ML prediction.

functions.name: mxgap.ML.process_edge_predictions
documentation: Process edge-based regressor predictions.

functions.name: mxgap.ML.handle_classifier_and_regressor
documentation: Handle cases where a classifier and a regressor are used together.

functions.name: mxgap.ML.handle_single_model
documentation: Handle cases where only a single model (classifier or regressor) is used.

functions.name: mxgap.ML.ML_prediction
documentation:
Main function for predicting bandgap with ML model, from CONTCAR and DOSCAR paths.

Parameters
-----------

`contcar_path`  : Path for the CONTCAR file.
`doscar_path`   : Path for the DOSCAR file.
`model`         : ML model to use. Defaults to GBC+RFR_onlygap (best).
`output`        : Path to save the output. Defaults to "mxgap.info" in the same directory as CONTCAR.

functions.name: mxgap.ML.run_prediction
documentation:
Main function for predicting bandgap with ML model. Does the validation of inputs.

Parameters
----------
`path`   : Optional. Path of the folder of a calculation, where the CONTCAR and DOSCAR are found. By default cwd.
`model`  : Optional. ML model to use. By default GBC+RFR_onlygap (best).
`files`  : Optional. Specify the paths for the CONTCAR and DOSCAR files, in a list. By default None. 
           Use either `paths` or `files`, if both are specified, `path` will take preference.
`output` : Optional. Specify the output file. By default it will generate a mxgap.info in the CONTCAR folder. 

Returns
---------
`pred` : Result of the prediction in a list. The length will vary depending on the used model. 
         Can be either 1 (single Classifier or Regressor), 2 for combination of C+R, or +2 more for each when using the R_edges approach.

modules.name: mxgap.structure
documentation:
Module for modifying structures and extracting the information in an automatic way.
This program is optimized for 2D MXene structures.

Diego Ontiveros

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.__init__
documentation:
Creates an ASE object with the information of the given geometry file.
Allows to modify its geometry with different functions and get parameters.
Optimized for slab MXene compounds. Inherits from ase.Atoms.

`path` : Path for to the geometry file. (e.g. CONTCAR)

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.write
documentation:
Writes the object to a geometry file. Any **kwargs will be passed to the ase.io.write() function.

`format`  : Str. Format of the geometry file. Accepts any of the ase.io.formats types. By default vasp.
`sortMXT` : Bool. For MXenes, if True, it follows M>X>T order on writing.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.get_geom
documentation:
Returns lattice parameter a and width d in Angstrom. 

Returns extra distances if extra=True (For terminated MXenes).

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.get_stack_hollows
documentation: Gets the stack and termination hollow position for pristine or terminated MXenes.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.get_n_terminations
documentation: Returns the number of terminations (0 pristine, 1 T, 2, OH, etc). (For MXenes)

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.get_repeated_T
documentation: Returns the number of repeated atoms in the termination with X.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.getMXT
documentation:
Get M, X, T positions or symbols in order (sorted by z). For terminated MXenes.

`scaled` : Bool. Will use scaled positions instead of absolute.
`symbols` : Bool. Will return chemical symbols instead of positions.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.sortMXT
documentation: Returns a M>X>T sorted version of the MXene.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.to_zero
documentation: Shifts positions of atoms to start at zero.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.shift
documentation:
Shifts the structure a given vector and applies PBC. 
If only one value is given, the z direction will be shifted.

`displacement` : Value or 3D vector for the shift.
`scaled` : Bool. If the vector is in fractional coordinates. By default True.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.add_vacuum
documentation: Adds the indicated vacuum to the slab.

methods.name: mxgap.structure.Structure.addT
documentation:
Adds single-atom termination (T) to the pristine MXene in the indicated hole position (hollows=HM/H,HMX,HX).

HMX uses HM and HX for the lower and upper surfaces, respectively. Use HMXi for the inverted configuration. (For MXenes).

`symbol` : Str. Chemical symbol of the termination.
`dist`   : Float. Distance from the added termination to the slab. Defaults to 1.
`hollow` : Str. Hollow position for the termination (HM/H, HMX, HX).

modules.name: mxgap.features
documentation:
Reads input files and extracts the feature array that will be passed to the ML model.

Diego Ontiveros

functions.name: mxgap.features.get_contcar_array
documentation: Gets the raw input data array from the CONTCAR file.

functions.name: mxgap.features.get_doscar_array
documentation: Gets the raw input data array from the DOSCAR file.

functions.name: mxgap.features.join_data_array
documentation: Joins the raw data arrays into the final normalized array that goes into the ML model.

functions.name: mxgap.features.make_data_array
documentation: Gets the normalized data_array from the input paths.

modules.name: mxgap.dos
documentation:
Script to analyze Density of States (DOS). 
For now, only VASP DOSCAR files are implemented.

Diego Ontiveros

functions.name: mxgap.dos.get_bandgap
documentation:
Gets the bandgap from the given E and DOS arrays. 
Assumes E is corrected with Ef.

functions.name: mxgap.dos.make_histogram
documentation:
Makes a histogram from the given E and DOS arrays.

`n_bins` : Number of histogram bins. By default 100.
`E_min`  : Minimum energy to start histogram. By default -5.
`E_max`  : Maximum energy to end histogram. By default +5.

methods.name: mxgap.dos.Doscar.__init__
documentation:
Main class to anaylze the DOS of a given file (path). 
Uses ASE VaspDos to read the DOSCAR.

methods.name: mxgap.dos.Doscar.get_dos
documentation:
Gets the DOS and DOS information from DOSCAR file.

Returns
-------
`E`     : Energy array of the DOS. Corrected with Ef.
`dos`   : Total DOS array. up+down if spin polarized.
`Ef`    : Fermi Level, in eV.
`ispin` : Number of spin channels (1 or 2).

methods.name: mxgap.dos.Doscar.get_bandgap
documentation: Gets the bandgap from the DOS. Assumes E is corrected with Ef.

methods.name: mxgap.dos.Doscar.make_histogram
documentation: Makes a histogram from the given DOS.
